rationale: In this editorial, the cited paper is not just listed in the references; it is explicitly discussed for its core result that retellings systematically diverge from original news stories. The authors also reuse it in multiple sections as an example of substantive insight (word of mouth distortion) and of multimedia/text-analytic research design in the special issue. This gives the citation a clear functional role in framing what multimedia marketing research can reveal, even though the editorial does not extend or test that paper directly.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Melumad, Meyer, and Kim (2021) as a concrete exemplar of how sequential retelling changes content and interpretation, supporting the special issue's framing of multimedia data opportunities. It also cites the study as an illustration of large-scale unstructured text collection and analysis in marketing research.
